summary: Read-only audit of durable source/provenance surfaces. Source chunks and provenance files are structurally present, but live graph health is stale against current accepted/index counts, and accepted claim-source linkage has a missing/non-path source plus no accepted records directly linked through recognized source fields to 30_Sources/chunks. Existing verifiers passed graph-health fixtures and public-data safety reported degraded warnings but no blockers.
decisions: Kept audit read-only for source files and did not edit application or durable memory records.
Treated source chunk records, .dino/provenance records, accepted instance source fields, wiki index, graph-health file, and verifier outputs as evidence.
Classified graph-health freshness and claim-source schema mismatch as blockers for durable provenance confidence.
next_steps: Refresh graph health from the current corpus after fixing schema/source linkage.
Normalize accepted memory source fields so source chunk paths are represented in fields graph-health reads, or teach graph-health to read linked_records.representative_source_chunks.
Replace prose evidence source in rag-public-os-learning-packet.json with an existing durable path or classify it outside source_mapping checks.
